response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad web and distributed systems background, with tangentially relevant geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map, and some exposure to high-throughput data pipelines at Dunnhumby. However, the role requires deep modern C++ or Rust expertise as a core requirement, and the candidate's C++ experience is limited to a brief six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. There is no Rust experience at all. Critically, the candidate has no experience with routing data pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or routing tile generation — the central responsibilities of this role. The primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not transfer to this position.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map (web GIS application), showing familiarity with map data concepts
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang applications
Strong polyglot and adaptability track record across many languages and environments over 15+ years
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015; bachelor thesis project), far below the commercial-grade modern C++ depth this role demands
No Rust experience whatsoever, eliminating the only acceptable alternative to C++
Zero experience with routing pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or routing tile generation — the core deliverables of this role
